title: Daily Reporting Output
description: Output shape produced by daily-reporting. Machine-readable mirror of references/schemas/output-schema.md. See ./MAINTAINING.md for sync discipline.
$comment: daily-reporting produces (1) a human-readable report and (2) a structured state artifact for continuity.

mode.description: Reporting cycle. Use mode consistently for sod or eod; do not introduce a second field like report_type for the same concept.

status.$comment: A populated missing_sources[] must be paired with a downgraded status ('partial', or 'blocked' when a required source is absent).

report_stage.description: Where the report is in the review flow.
report_stage.$comment: generated: draft produced, not yet reviewed. reviewed: reviewed (with or without edits) but not yet locked. locked: confirmed and locked for continuity. sent: locked final delivered to delivery_meta.final_destination.

finalization_mode.description: Which finalization policy the deployment applied.
finalization_mode.$comment: Echo of review_policy.finalization_mode (see ../policies/review-and-locking.md). Present once the report reaches 'locked' or 'sent'. report_stage and finalization_mode are orthogonal and must not be conflated.

human_readable_report.description: Polished narrative rendering of merged_context per the SOD/EOD templates.
human_readable_report.$comment: May polish narrative slots. Three narrative slots are explicitly permitted to be polished/synthesized at draft time: {{priority_context_summary}} (SOD), {{proposed_focus_statement}} (SOD), {{day_verdict_summary}} (EOD). Every other slot must be rendered verbatim from the merged_context field named in the template's 'Slot-to-field reference' table. See output-schema.md 'Polish rule' and 'Polish-slot verification'.

owner_type.description: Role identifier vocabulary. Canonical declaration lives in deployment-config-schema.md.

participant.description: Canonical participant shape mirror. Source-of-truth is deployment-config-schema.md.

structured_state.description: Structured state artifact written for continuity.
structured_state.$comment: structured_state fields must be copied verbatim from merged_context. No re-wording, no new items, no synthesized narrative. If merged_context does not contain content for a structured_state field, omit the field or emit an empty array - never synthesize new content directly into structured_state.

confirmed_by.description: Must match one of participants[].name in the same structured_state.
confirmed_by.$comment: Identifies the specific person by display name, not the role.

confirmed_role.description: Role under which the confirmation occurred.
confirmed_role.$comment: Must match the owner_type of the participant whose name equals confirmed_by.

locked_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of transition to report_stage: locked.

delivery_meta.description: Records the delivery destinations used for this run.
delivery_meta.$comment: Keys echo deployment-config's delivery_policy with a naming adjustment: delivery_policy.draft_destination is recorded here as review_destination.

review_destination.description: Destination identifier used for drafts and in-review revisions. Matches deployment_config.defaults.delivery_policy.draft_destination (renamed here to describe the runtime role of the surface).

final_destination.description: Destination identifier used for locked finals. Matches deployment_config.defaults.delivery_policy.final_destination.

validation_meta.description: Helps a client or tool understand non-fatal issues.

schema_version.description: Schema version the run asserted for its output shape.

warnings.description: List of non-fatal warning entries surfaced during the run.
warnings.$comment: Warning shape is intentionally loose in v1: string or object. Runtimes may encode warnings as plain strings (e.g. the sod-reviewed-locked example uses an empty array) or richer objects.

missing_sources.description: Source families that did not return usable data for reasons other than being intentionally disabled.
missing_sources.$comment: Populate missing_sources[] only for case (b) in skills/daily-reporting/SKILL.md ('If data is missing') - connector error, timeout, auth failure, or invalid payload. Do not populate it for case (a) (zero items returned successfully) or case (c) (connector intentionally disabled). A populated missing_sources[] must be paired with a corresponding warnings[] entry and a downgraded status.

missing_source_entry.description: Records one source family that did not contribute usable data.

source_family.description: The affected source key. Must be one of the canonical source keys in deployment-config-schema.md §Source keys.

reason.description: Why the source did not contribute.
reason.$comment: error: connector returned an error. timeout: connector exceeded its retrieval window. auth_failure: authentication or authorization failed. invalid_payload: connector returned data that failed schema validation, including a corrupted prior locked state per ../policies/continuity-model.md. required_but_absent: source is marked required in the deployment config but was unreachable and no fallback exists.
